  "text": "The OpenJDK security provider registration framework is implemented across multiple Java source files in the java.base module. The core framework consists of: (1) java/security/Provider.java - the abstract base class that all security providers inherit from, which includes the putService() method (line 1221) used to register cryptographic services; (2) sun/security/jca/ProviderConfig.java - the lazy-loading configuration mechanism that maps provider names to provider classes and handles the runtime loading of providers; (3) sun/security/jca/ProviderList.java - manages the list of available providers and their precedence; and (4) sun/security/jca/Providers.java - provides the system-wide provider list management and specifies the default providers (SUN, SunRsaSign, SunEC, SunJCE) to be used for JAR verification. The provider implementations include: (1) sun/security/provider/Sun.java - the Sun provider extending Provider class that implements core algorithms like DSA, SHA, MD5, and X.509 certificate support, using SunEntries to define and register its services via putService(); (2) sun/security/provider/SunEntries.java - defines the actual service entries (algorithms, key factories, keystores, certificate validators) for the Sun provider; (3) com/sun/crypto/provider/SunJCE.java - the SunJCE provider extending Provider that registers cryptographic algorithms including RSA encryption, DES, AES, ChaCha20, various cipher modes, HMAC variants, and password-based encryption; (4) sun/security/ssl/SunJSSE.java - the SunJSSE provider extending Provider that registers SSL/TLS-related services including the KeyManagerFactory and TrustManagerFactory for PKCS12, SunX509, and other key/trust mechanisms; and (5) sun/security/ec/SunEC.java - the SunEC provider extending Provider that registers services for elliptic curve cryptography including EdDSA signatures and EC key factories. This architecture allows Java applications to dynamically request cryptographic and security services through a pluggable provider framework, with providers loaded on-demand through the ProviderConfig lazy-loading mechanism."
}

## Your Task

Find all Java source files in openjdk/jdk that implement the security provider registration framework. ## Core Framework Components

**Framework Base:**
- **`java/security/Provider.java`** - The abstract base class that all security providers extend from, containing the critical `putService()` method (line 1221) for registering cryptographic services.

**Provider Management:**
- **`sun/security/jca/ProviderConfig.java`** - Implements the lazy-loading mechanism that maps provider names to provider classes and handles runtime provider instantiation.
- **`sun/security/jca/ProviderList.java`** - Manages the ordered list of available providers and their precedence.
- **`sun/security/jca/Providers.java`** - System-wide provider list management, specifying default providers (SUN, SunRsaSign, SunEC, SunJCE).

## Provider Implementations

1. **`sun/security/provider/Sun.java`** - Core provider implementing DSA, SHA, MD5, X.509 certificates, and keystores
   - Uses **`sun/security/provider/SunEntries.java`** to define and register services

2. **`com/sun/crypto/provider/SunJCE.java`** - Cryptographic algorithms (RSA, DES, AES, ChaCha20, HMAC, PBE)

3. **`sun/security/ssl/SunJSSE.java`** - SSL/TLS support with KeyManagerFactory and TrustManagerFactory

4. **`sun/security/ec/SunEC.java`** - Elliptic curve cryptography algorithms

  "text": "The OpenJDK security provider registration framework is implemented across multiple Java source files in the java.base module. The core framework consists of: (1) java/security/Provider.java - the abstract base class that all security providers inherit from, which includes the putService() method (line 1221) used to register cryptographic services; (2) sun/security/jca/ProviderConfig.java - the lazy-loading configuration mechanism that maps provider names to provider classes and handles the runtime loading of providers; (3) sun/security/jca/ProviderList.java - manages the list of available providers and their precedence; and (4) sun/security/jca/Providers.java - provides the system-wide provider list management and specifies the default providers (SUN, SunRsaSign, SunEC, SunJCE) to be used for JAR verification. The provider implementations include: (1) sun/security/provider/Sun.java - the Sun provider extending Provider class that implements core algorithms like DSA, SHA, MD5, and X.509 certificate support, using SunEntries to define and register its services via putService(); (2) sun/security/provider/SunEntries.java - defines the actual service entries (algorithms, key factories, keystores, certificate validators) for the Sun provider; (3) com/sun/crypto/provider/SunJCE.java - the SunJCE provider extending Provider that registers cryptographic algorithms including RSA encryption, DES, AES, ChaCha20, various cipher modes, HMAC variants, and password-based encryption; (4) sun/security/ssl/SunJSSE.java - the SunJSSE provider extending Provider that registers SSL/TLS-related services including the KeyManagerFactory and TrustManagerFactory for PKCS12, SunX509, and other key/trust mechanisms; and (5) sun/security/ec/SunEC.java - the SunEC provider extending Provider that registers services for elliptic curve cryptography including EdDSA signatures and EC key factories. This architecture allows Java applications to dynamically request cryptographic and security services through a pluggable provider framework, with providers loaded on-demand through the ProviderConfig lazy-loading mechanism."
}

The provider implementations include: (1) sun/security/provider/Sun.java - the Sun provider extending Provider class that implements core algorithms like DSA, SHA, MD5, and X.509 certificate support, using SunEntries to define and register its services via putService(); (2) sun/security/provider/SunEntries.java - defines the actual service entries (algorithms, key factories, keystores, certificate validators) for the Sun provider; (3) com/sun/crypto/provider/SunJCE.java - the SunJCE provider extending Provider that registers cryptographic algorithms including RSA encryption, DES, AES, ChaCha20, various cipher modes, HMAC variants, and password-based encryption; (4) sun/security/ssl/SunJSSE.java - the SunJSSE provider extending Provider that registers SSL/TLS-related services including the KeyManagerFactory and TrustManagerFactory for PKCS12, SunX509, and other key/trust mechanisms; and (5) sun/security/ec/SunEC.java - the SunEC provider extending Provider that registers services for elliptic curve cryptography including EdDSA signatures and EC key factories.
